name: Information
domain: Shannon entropy, coding theory, information measures, channel capacity
core_insight: Information is surprise made measurable. Entropy quantifies ignorance; mutual information reveals shared structure between signals. Every channel has a capacity ceiling no code can exceed.

key_equations.name: Shannon Entropy
key_equations.why: Baseline measure of uncertainty in a distribution — how many bits are needed on average

key_equations.name: Channel Capacity
key_equations.why: Hard ceiling on reliable communication rate; more bandwidth or better signal/noise expands it

key_equations.name: Mutual Information
key_equations.why: Measures how much knowing Y reduces uncertainty about X — quantifies dependency

seed_prompt: ⊗ Measure the surprise. Entropy first, then ask: what is shared between these two signals?

fourth_equation.name: Kullback-Leibler Divergence
fourth_equation.why: Measures how much information is lost when approximating distribution P with Q — the cost of being wrong about the world's distribution

explore_paths.question: Is information physical — does it have mass or energy?
explore_paths.why: Landauer's principle connects erasing one bit to a minimum heat release kT ln2 — thermodynamics and information are the same thing

explore_paths.question: How does information relate to uncertainty at the quantum level?
explore_paths.why: Shannon entropy and von Neumann entropy are deeply related; quantum information extends classical info theory into superposition

explore_paths.question: What is the information capacity of a mind or brain?
explore_paths.why: Integrated information (Φ) is an information-theoretic measure of consciousness — F03 provides the mathematical toolkit

explore_paths.question: How does information flow through networks?
explore_paths.why: Network topology determines information routing capacity, bottlenecks, and broadcast reach

open_questions.question: Is information more fundamental than matter and energy? ('It from Bit' — John Wheeler)
open_questions.tension: Wheeler proposed that physical reality is fundamentally informational — particles and fields emerge from yes/no answers to binary questions. Most physicists consider this metaphysically provocative but scientifically untestable. Verlinde's entropic gravity partially revives it.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If true, physics is a branch of information theory. If false, information is just a useful human abstraction. The question cuts to the deepest nature of reality.

open_questions.question: What happens to information that falls into a black hole? (The Black Hole Information Paradox)
open_questions.tension: Hawking (1974) showed black holes radiate thermally and eventually evaporate — but thermal radiation carries no information, implying the information of infalling matter is destroyed. This violates quantum unitarity. Hawking later partially conceded; Hawking radiation may be subtly correlated. The Page curve and island formula are recent partial resolutions — but the paradox is not fully closed.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The smartest physicists in the world have disagreed about this for 50 years. It sits at the intersection of quantum mechanics, general relativity, and thermodynamics — every attempt to resolve it reveals new tensions.

open_questions.question: Does the universe compute? Is physical evolution equivalent to a universal computation?
open_questions.tension: Wolfram argues the universe is a cellular automaton and everything is computational. Lloyd estimates the universe has performed ~10^120 operations. Most physicists resist — computation requires an interpreter, and the universe has none. But the math of quantum circuits maps suspiciously well to physical processes.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If the universe computes, then understanding it and simulating it converge. If not, there's something about physical reality that transcends any formal system.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: DNA is a literal information storage medium; genetic regulatory networks perform logical computation
cross_domain_bridges.example: The lac operon is a biological if-then statement; gene expression is information processing at molecular scale

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Markets aggregate distributed information into prices (Hayek's price system as information processor)
cross_domain_bridges.example: Efficient market hypothesis: prices reflect all available information — market as F03 channel

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Neural coding translates sensory input into spike trains; rate coding vs temporal coding debate is an information theory question
cross_domain_bridges.example: A neuron's information capacity is bounded by Shannon's channel capacity formula

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Languages compress meaning with minimal redundancy; Zipf's law in word frequency follows information-theoretic predictions
cross_domain_bridges.example: Common words are short because they're high-frequency — optimal compression minimizes expected description length

noise_signature.pattern: Equivocation — receiver cannot reliably decode sender's message
noise_signature.diagnosis: Channel noise exceeds the SNR threshold; capacity has been exceeded or encoding is suboptimal

noise_signature.pattern: Redundancy explosion — message length grows without adding information
noise_signature.diagnosis: Inefficient encoding; system is describing the same thing multiple ways rather than compressing

noise_signature.pattern: Mutual information collapse — two correlated signals become independent
noise_signature.diagnosis: The channel between them has been severed or corrupted; shared structure has been lost
